definition: To explain something clearly, step by step
example: Let me walk you through the approach before writing any code.

definition: To explain a benefit vs cost decision
example: The trade-off here is time complexity versus space complexity.

definition: To flag a boundary condition in a solution
example: One edge case to consider is when the input array is empty.

definition: To explain your first instinct or approach
example: My initial thought was to use brute force with O(n²) complexity.

word: a more optimal approach would be
definition: To introduce a better solution than what was just discussed
example: A more optimal approach would be to use a hashmap here.

definition: To extend or expand a previous point
example: To build on that, we can also cache results to avoid recomputation.

word: if I understand the problem correctly
definition: To confirm your understanding before solving
example: If I understand the problem correctly, we need the shortest path.

word: the reason I chose this approach is
definition: To justify a technical decision clearly
example: The reason I chose this approach is that it avoids extra space.

definition: To test or validate the approach against examples
example: To verify my solution, let me trace through a sample input.

definition: To check that the listener has understood your explanation
example: We use two pointers to avoid nested loops. Does that make sense?

word: breaking this down into smaller steps
definition: To simplify a complex problem
example: Breaking this down into smaller steps, we first sort the array.

definition: To focus on improving execution speed
example: To optimize for time, we will sacrifice some memory by using a set.

definition: Used to establish a plan first
example: Before we write any code, let's agree on the data structures.

definition: Identifying the slowest part of a system
example: The bottleneck of this approach is the nested loop searching.

definition: To look at the broader picture or reconsider
example: Taking a step back, maybe a graph traversal isn't needed here.

definition: To mentally trace through the code
example: Let's dry run this logic with an input of [1, 2, 3].

word: another way to look at this
definition: Introducing an alternative perspective
example: Another way to look at this is as a dynamic programming problem.

definition: Describing the maximum possible complexity
example: In the worst case scenario, we traverse every node in the tree.

definition: A simple, but usually inefficient, first attempt
example: A naive implementation would check every possible substring.

definition: To explain something in easy terms
example: To put it simply, it behaves like a First-In-First-Out queue.
